AuthStatusPhase.description:
Public auth status phase shared by REST, RPC, CLI, and generated SDK wire
payloads.

This is the compatibility projection of typed auth lease truth. Surfaces
should map to this enum first and only then emit [`Self::as_public_str`].

BindingId.description: Opaque slug identifying a binding inside a realm.

BlobId.description:
Canonical realm-local blob identifier.

The identifier is content-addressed, but storage and GC semantics remain
realm-scoped.

BlobRef.description: Durable image reference owned by transcript/runtime state.

BridgeBootstrapToken.description:
One-time bootstrap proof exchanged between a mob supervisor and a
member runtime on initial bind.

Transparent over the wire (`#[serde(transparent)]` — a bare JSON string),
but carries a redacting `Debug` impl and has no `Display` impl so the
raw secret cannot accidentally land in logs or panic messages. Treat it
like an API key: read `as_str()` only at the comms/transport boundary.

BridgeCapabilities.description: Capabilities advertised by a member runtime on bind.

current_protocol_version.description: Protocol version implemented by the responding member runtime.

default_protocol_version.description: Protocol version new supervisors should use for fresh authority records.

supported_protocol_versions.description: Protocol versions accepted by the responding member runtime.

BridgeCommand.description: A typed command sent from a supervisor to a member runtime.

oneOf.description: Bind a remote runtime to this supervisor.

expected_peer_id.description: Expected canonical member `PeerId`; not an Ed25519 public-key string.

oneOf.description: Supervisor authority credentials included in every bridge command.
Supervisor authority credentials included in every bridge command.
Deliver one logical input to a member.
Supervisor authority credentials included in every bridge command.
Supervisor authority credentials included in every bridge command.

Explicit hard-cancel command payload.

`InterruptMember` is the cooperative boundary-break path. This payload is
intentionally separate so supervisors cannot accidentally collapse boundary
cancellation and immediate user/session interrupt authority onto one wire
command.
Supervisor authority credentials included in every bridge command.
Supervisor authority credentials included in every bridge command.
Peer wiring command payload.
Peer wiring command payload.

BridgeCommand.title: BridgeCommand

BridgeDeliveryRejectionCause.description:
Typed vocabulary for why member input delivery was rejected.

This mirrors the runtime accept-boundary rejection vocabulary at the
bridge wire boundary. Callers should branch on this typed cause and treat
the sibling `reason` string on [`BridgeDeliveryOutcome::Rejected`] as
operator-facing presentation only.

oneOf.description: Runtime was not in a state that accepts input.
Input failed durability validation.
Peer input carried a forbidden handling mode.
The bridge could not map the rejection to a known typed cause.

BridgeMemberRuntimeState.description: Wire projection of a member's runtime state.

BridgePeerConnectivity.description: Connectivity class observed for the bridged member runtime.

BridgePeerSpec.description:
Minimal trusted peer identity for supervisor bridge wire messages.

Mirrors `meerkat_core::comms::TrustedPeerDescriptor` (post-C-TRP) but is
self-contained in the contracts crate so neither sender nor receiver
needs a cross-crate dependency for deserialization. Fields stay
stringly at the wire boundary — `peer_id` is the canonical comms routing
UUID, while raw Ed25519 public key material is carried only in `pubkey`.
The typed `PeerId`/`PeerName`/`PeerAddress` atoms are re-hydrated on the
receiving side.

pubkey.description:
Ed25519 signing public key bytes — required so the receiver can
verify envelope signatures after trust registration. Serialized
as a 32-element array; the JSON form is an array of numbers, not
a base64/hex string, to keep the wire deliberately boring.

BridgeProtocolVersion.description:
A supported supervisor bridge wire protocol version.

The JSON representation remains the historic integer so persisted records
and wire payloads do not change shape. Construction is intentionally routed
through this type so unsupported values fail at serde/TryFrom boundaries
instead of being carried deeper as raw integers.

BridgeRejectionCause.description:
Typed vocabulary for why a bridge command was rejected.

Callers branch on the typed `cause` to drive recovery logic; the
accompanying `reason` string is for operator diagnostics only and must
not be pattern-matched. Reserve `Internal` for true invariant
violations — ordinary validation failures get a specific cause.

oneOf.description: No supervisor is bound yet; caller must `bind_member` first.
The request targets an older epoch than the bound authority.
The authenticated sender does not match the authorized supervisor.

A different supervisor is already bound; rotation must go through
`authorize_supervisor`, not `bind_member`.
The bootstrap token did not match the runtime's expected value.
The wire protocol version is not supported by this runtime.
The embedded supervisor peer spec failed validation.
The embedded trusted-peer spec failed validation.

The `expected_address` in the bind payload does not match this
runtime's advertised address.
The command variant is not currently handled by this runtime.
An unexpected invariant was violated while handling the command.

BridgeRejectionCause.title: BridgeRejectionCause

BridgeReply.description: A typed reply from a member runtime back to the supervisor.

oneOf.description: Response to a bind command.

peer_id.description: Canonical member `PeerId`; transport public-key bytes stay out of this field.

oneOf.description: Simple acknowledgment.
Response to an observe command.

current_run_id.description: Current run identifier reported by the member runtime.

state.description: Bridged runtime state for the observed member.

oneOf.description: Full response to a delivery command.
Response to a retire command.
Response to a destroy command.

BridgeReply.title: BridgeReply

CapabilityEntry.description: A single capability's status in the current runtime.

CapabilityHint.description: Hint about which capability is needed.
CapabilityHint.title: CapabilityHint

CapabilityId.description:
Slug-validated capability identifier for skill requirements.

Replaces the legacy `Vec<String>` capability lists with a typed
namespace. Parsed at construction, so callers cannot smuggle invalid
identifiers into descriptors or requirements.

oneOf.description: Compiled in, config-enabled, protocol supports it.

Compiled in but disabled by policy.

`description` summarizes why — this is intentionally a human-readable
string, not a config path, because the reality is multi-layered:
always-compiled features like `Builtins` and `Shell` are controlled by
`AgentFactory` flags, per-build `AgentBuildConfig` overrides, and
`BuiltinToolConfig` policy layers (soft + enforced). A single
"config_path" can't represent that resolution chain.
Not compiled into this build (feature flag absent).
This protocol surface doesn't support it.

CatalogModelEntry.description: A single model entry in the catalog response.

context_window.description: Maximum input context window in tokens.

profile.description: Model profile with capability flags and parameter schema.

server_id.description: Backing self-hosted server ID for configured aliases.

CatalogModelEntry.title: CatalogModelEntry

CommsChecksumTokenParams.description:
Typed params for the actionable checksum-token request used by peer
request/response terminal-flow fixtures.
CommsChecksumTokenParams.title: CommsChecksumTokenParams

CommsChecksumTokenResult.description: Typed result for a checksum-token peer response.
CommsChecksumTokenResult.title: CommsChecksumTokenResult

CommsPeerLifecycleParams.description:
Typed params for one-way peer lifecycle notifications.

This is the public wire projection of the topology-update payloads that
used to travel as arbitrary JSON. `peer_spec` is the canonical typed
identity when the sender has it; `peer`, `role`, and `description` remain
inert presentation metadata for older projections.

CommsPeerRequestIntent.description:
Closed public request-intent contract for `peer_request`.

Unknown strings fail during deserialization and cannot fall through to a
local match/default path.

CommsPeerResponseResult.description:
Typed result payload for public `peer_response`.

Compatibility JSON is intentionally not accepted here. Callers that include
a `result` field must provide a typed bridge reply shape.

oneOf.description: Plain text content.
Base64-encoded image content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.
Durable blob reference used by persisted history/runtime state.
Base64-encoded video content.
Base64-encoded inline bytes used for ingress and live execution.

ContentInput.description:
Input content that can be either a plain text string or multimodal content blocks.

Deserializes from either `"text"` or `[{type: "text", text: "..."}, ...]`.
Provides `From<String>` and `From<&str>` for ergonomic construction.

ContractVersion.description:
Semantic version for the Meerkat wire contract.

Start at 0.1.0 — allow breaking changes during Phase 1-3 development.
Bump to 1.0.0 when the SDK Builder ships (Phase 5) and external
consumers exist. Before 1.0.0, minor bumps can be breaking.
ContractVersion.title: ContractVersion

ErrorCode.description: Stable error codes for wire protocol.
ErrorCode.title: ErrorCode

HandlingMode.description:
Handling mode for ordinary content-bearing work.

`Queue` means outer-loop / next-turn handling and leaves the current run
untouched.
`Steer` means inner-loop handling and requests injection at the earliest
admissible cooperative boundary, while remaining ordinary work rather than
an out-of-band control-plane command.

role_name.description: Role name (profile key) for the helper in the mob roster.

InputSource.description: Source for an input event posted to an agent.

InputStreamMode.description: Whether this input/peer command should reserve a local interaction stream.

oneOf.description: Do not reserve any stream.
Reserve an interaction stream for the command.

McpHttpConfig.description: HTTP transport configuration (streamable HTTP or legacy SSE)

headers.description: Extra headers to include on requests

McpHttpTransport.description: HTTP transport selection for URL-based servers

McpLiveOpResponse.description: Response payload for live MCP operations.
McpLiveOpResponse.title: McpLiveOpResponse

McpLiveOpStatus.description: Shared status for live MCP operations.

McpLiveOperation.description: Shared operation kind for live MCP operations.

McpServerConfig.description: Configuration for a single MCP server

connect_timeout_secs.description:
Connection timeout in seconds (connect + handshake + list_tools).
Defaults to 10 seconds when not specified.

name.description: Server name (must be unique within scope)

args.description: Arguments to pass to the command

oneOf.description:
Skip materially late pending occurrences instead of catching them up.

The scheduler applies a small internal grace window so normal tick
jitter does not immediately misfire on-time work.

Catch up overdue pending occurrences only while they remain within the
configured lateness window.

ModelsCatalogResponse.description: Response for `models/catalog` — the compiled-in model catalog.
ModelsCatalogResponse.title: ModelsCatalogResponse

OccurrenceId.description: Opaque identifier for a persisted occurrence.

OutputSchema.description:
Configuration for structured output extraction.

When provided to an agent, the agent will perform an extraction turn after
completing the agentic work, forcing the LLM to output validated JSON that
conforms to the provided schema. The extraction JSON becomes the final
response text (schema-only) in [`RunResult`].

compat.description: Compatibility mode for provider lowering (lossy or strict)

name.description: Optional name for the schema (used by some providers)

schema.description: The JSON schema that the output must conform to

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict schema validation (provider-specific)

PeerAddress.description:
Typed peer address: transport atom plus endpoint string.

The `endpoint` is transport-specific (path for `Uds`, `host:port` for
`Tcp`, agent name for `Inproc`) but is carried as a validated `String`
so the transport atom can be branched on without re-parsing.

PeerCapabilitySet.description:
Typed peer capability envelope for peer-directory output.

Extensions are intentionally opaque display/integration metadata. Core
routing, admission, and policy decisions must use typed fields such as
[`PeerDirectoryEntry::sendable_kinds`] instead of consulting this bag.

PeerCorrelationId.description:
Canonical correlation identifier for the peer request/response lifecycle.

On the sender side, `PeerCorrelationId` is both the outbound request
envelope id and the reservation key for any stream subscription. On the
receiver side, every response envelope carries it as `in_reply_to`. The
sender routes a reply back to its originating request by matching on this
id — no local id-translation map.

Wrapping it in a newtype prevents raw-`Uuid` confusion at every site that
fires a DSL input, inserts into the subscriber registry, or closes out an
inbound request.

address.description:
Typed transport atom + endpoint. Replaces the prior free-form
`address: String` so the transport cannot be invented by string
concatenation at a call site.

name.description:
Display-only slug. Multiple entries may share a name; none share a
`peer_id`.

peer_id.description: Canonical runtime identity — the routing key.

PeerId.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a peer.

`PeerId` is the routing key: the router and trust store key by `PeerId`,
never by `PeerName`. Two peers may legitimately share a display `PeerName`
(per the Wave-B V5 dogma note), but their `PeerId`s never collide — the
underlying UUID is globally unique.

Constructed freshly (`PeerId::new`) for a peer minted locally, parsed
from a hyphenated UUID (`PeerId::parse`) when we've been given an identity
over the wire, or derived from a 32-byte Ed25519 public key when a transport
still authenticates by raw signing key.

PeerLifecycleKind.description:
One-way peer lifecycle notification kind.

These notifications are control-plane topology updates, not correlated
peer work requests. They intentionally do not create request/response
lifecycles and must never require an LLM-authored reply.

PeerMeta.description:
Supplementary metadata attached to a peer for discovery.

The peer's routing name lives in `TrustedPeer.name` / `comms_name`.
`PeerMeta` carries additional context: what the agent does and
arbitrary key/value labels.

# Serde

Both fields use `skip_serializing_if`, so `PeerMeta::default()`
serializes to `{}`.

description.description: What this agent does (e.g. "Reviews pull requests for style issues").

labels.description:
Arbitrary key/value labels (e.g. `{"lang": "rust", "team": "infra"}`).

`BTreeMap` keeps keys sorted for deterministic serialization.

PeerName.description:
Display-only slug for a peer.

`PeerName` is **not** a routing key after Wave-B V5: the router resolves
sends by [`PeerId`], and trust stores are keyed by [`PeerId`]. `PeerName`
is retained so human-facing surfaces (CLI, REST `comms.peers`, logs) can
render a recognisable handle next to the opaque id.

oneOf.description:
The peer admitted the transport but rejected our envelope at its
ingress policy gate (untrusted sender, full inbox, etc.). The peer
is still reachable at the transport level; policy denied us.

PeerResponseTerminalStatusWire.description: Terminal status for dedicated correlated peer-response ingress.
PeerResponseTerminalStatusWire.title: PeerResponseTerminalStatusWire

PeerTransport.description:
Typed transport atom for a peer address.

Replaces the old free-form `address: String` on `PeerDirectoryEntry` so
callers cannot accidentally invent new transports by string concatenation
at a call site.

oneOf.description: In-process routing within this runtime (no network hop).
Unix domain socket.
TCP endpoint.

ProfileId.description: Opaque slug identifying an auth profile override on a connection.

ProviderCatalog.description: Provider-level grouping in the catalog response.

default_model_id.description: Default model ID for this provider.

models.description: All catalog models for this provider.

RealtimeAudioFormat.description: Descriptor for an expected or actual realtime audio format.

RealtimeCapabilities.description: Product-facing realtime capability set for one target/provider combination.

audio_input_format.description:
Audio format the provider session accepts for client audio input.
Clients MUST stamp incoming [`RealtimeAudioChunk`]s with the same
`sample_rate_hz` and `channels`; mismatches are rejected server-side.

audio_output_format.description: Audio format the provider session emits for output audio chunks.

RealtimeCapabilities.title: RealtimeCapabilities

RealtimeChannelConfig.description:
Per-channel runtime knobs negotiated at open time.

Additive fields only — clients that do not carry this struct inherit the
server-default behavior via `#[serde(default)]` on the parent
[`RealtimeOpenRequest`].

tool_timeout.description: Realtime-specific timeout policy for product-session tool dispatch.

RealtimeChannelRole.description: Opening role for a realtime channel.

RealtimeChannelState.description: Lifecycle state for a realtime channel.

deadline_at.description:
Wall-clock deadline after which the reconnect cycle will be abandoned
and the channel will close with `ReconnectExhausted`. Derived from the
reconnect policy's `max_total_ms` on top of the cycle's start time. RFC
3339 encoded, matches `next_retry_at`. Present only while the channel
is actively reconnecting.

RealtimeChannelTarget.description:
Target for a public realtime channel.

Two variants, one for each addressing mode:

- `SessionTarget` — standalone sessions (no mob-member continuity). The
  session id is pinned for the channel's lifetime; when that session
  ends, the channel ends.

- `MobMember` — mob-member continuity (W3-H / dogma #4). Identity is the
  canonical anchor, and the server resolves the current bridge session
  on every tick from the MobMachine's `member_session_bindings` map.
  Respawn atomically rotates the bridge session via the
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: Some }` effect; the
  channel survives without any SDK round-trip. A terminal
  `MemberSessionBindingChanged { old: Some, new: None }` closes the
  channel with `RealtimeErrorCode::BindingReleased`.
RealtimeChannelTarget.title: RealtimeChannelTarget

RealtimeInputKind.description: Input modality kind supported by a realtime channel.

channel_config.description:
Optional per-channel runtime knobs (tool budget, etc.). Omitted means
"use server defaults".

RealtimeOutputKind.description: Output modality kind supported by a realtime channel.

RealtimeProtocolVersion.description:
Canonical wire protocol version for realtime channels.

The version is bumped every time the on-the-wire shape of
[`RealtimeAudioChunk`], frame enums, or other load-bearing realtime
contracts changes in an incompatible way. Clients and servers handshake on
the string form; this enum is the single typed owner of the set.

oneOf.description:
v2: adds `sample_rate_hz` + `channels` to [`RealtimeAudioChunk`] and
typed [`RealtimeErrorCode`] on channel errors.

RealtimeReconnectPolicy.description: Public reconnect policy for a realtime channel.
RealtimeReconnectPolicy.title: RealtimeReconnectPolicy

oneOf.description: Use the server's default realtime tool timeout.

Do not apply a realtime-specific timeout. The runtime/tool dispatcher
may still enforce its own normal execution policy.
Apply this finite realtime-specific timeout.

RealtimeTurningMode.description: Turning mode for a realtime channel.

RenderClass.description:
Standardized rendering class for injected ordinary work.

This metadata is for normalization/injection rendering, not for core
runtime scheduling. It lets the system consistently frame peer messages,
external events, tool-scope notices, and similar inputs when they are
rendered into model-visible context.

RenderMetadata.description: Optional rendering metadata carried alongside ordinary work content.

RenderSalience.description:
Constrained salience metadata for injected ordinary work.

This is intentionally a small closed enum so call sites do not invent
arbitrary free-text urgency language.

ResponseStatus.description:
Typed status for response interactions.

Mirrors `CommsStatus` from `meerkat-comms` — the comms runtime converts at the boundary.

RuntimeHostCapabilities.description: Runtime capability surface for a host.
RuntimeHostCapabilities.title: RuntimeHostCapabilities

RuntimeHostEndpointProjection.description: Endpoint metadata that reports surface reachability without owning it.
RuntimeHostEndpointProjection.title: RuntimeHostEndpointProjection

RuntimeHostFeatureFlags.description: Existing host capability facts exposed as typed booleans.

RuntimeHostHealth.description: Runtime health projection for a host.
RuntimeHostHealth.title: RuntimeHostHealth

RuntimeHostHealthStatus.description: Health state of the host process as observed by the reporting surface.

RuntimeHostIdScope.description: Scope of stability for a reported runtime host id.

oneOf.description: Stable for the current runtime process only.
Derived from an explicit realm and instance pairing.

RuntimeHostIdScope.title: RuntimeHostIdScope

RuntimeHostInfo.description: Read-only host information. This is a projection, not a registry entry.
RuntimeHostInfo.title: RuntimeHostInfo

RuntimeHostRealmProjection.description: Realm/config metadata projected from the owning config store.

RuntimeStateResult.description: Response payload for runtime-backed session status projections.
RuntimeStateResult.title: RuntimeStateResult

ScheduleId.description: Opaque identifier for a persisted schedule.

SchemaFormat.description: Schema format versions supported by Meerkat.

SessionId.description: Unique identifier for a session (UUID v7 for time-ordering)

SkillEntry.description: Wire representation of a skill entry (for list responses).

is_active.description: Whether this skill is active (not shadowed by a higher-precedence source).

shadowed_by.description: If shadowed, the higher-precedence source identity.

SkillEntry.title: SkillEntry

SkillKey.description:
Canonical runtime identity for a skill.

This is the single identity carried across every surface — the wire parses
directly into this struct, tools receive this struct, the registry stores
this struct. There is no slash-delimited string path form.

SkillListResponse.description: Wire response for listing skills with introspection data.
SkillListResponse.title: SkillListResponse

SkillRef.description:
Structured skill reference for wire/surface input.

Post-V4 this has exactly one variant — `Structured` — and is kept only as a
typed enum to leave room for future source-scoped variants (e.g. alias or
capability-scoped forms) without changing the trait surface. No legacy
string form, no slash-delimited path, no untagged fallback.

SkillRuntimeDiagnostics.description: Runtime-visible diagnostics for skill health and quarantined entries.

SkillSourceProvenance.description:
Typed source provenance for a skill entry. `display_name` is presentation
metadata only; `source_uuid` is the stable identity.

SourceHealthSnapshot.description: Source health snapshot reported by sources.

SourceIdentityStatus.description: Source lifecycle status in the identity registry.

SourceTransportKind.description: Source transport class used for identity governance.

SystemNoticeKind.description: Typed system notice content carried in the transcript.

oneOf.description:
Auth lease transitioned to `reauth_required` — the active
`auth_binding` needs manual re-authentication before the next LLM
call can proceed (Phase 1.5-rev).

ToolAccessPolicy.description: Tool access control for delegated branches

oneOf.description: Inherit all tools from parent
Only allow specific tools
Block specific tools

ToolName.description:
Canonical typed name for an LLM-callable tool.

The model-facing wire shape is still the provider's string tool name, but
internal policy, routing, and registry surfaces carry this handle so raw
strings do not become the tool identity owner.

TurnTerminalCauseKind.description: Closed machine-owned classifier for why a turn reached a terminal failure.

WireAssistantBlock.description:
Transcript block inside a block-assistant message.

Not `PartialEq`: `ToolUse.args` is `Box<RawValue>` for pass-through
fidelity (core invariant — opaque from provider to dispatcher), and
`RawValue` does not derive equality. Equivalence checks should
round-trip through serialization and compare the serialized bytes.

args.description:
Wire-projected tool-call args. Carries the opaque JSON payload
as `Box<RawValue>` to mirror the core `AssistantBlock::ToolUse`
invariant ("tool-call args pass through un-parsed from provider
to dispatcher") — see `CLAUDE.md` Rust design principles §3.
Allow-listed by dogma-blind-spots §7.

WireAssistantBlock.title: WireAssistantBlock

WireAuthBindingRef.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::AuthBindingRef`].

Pure structural shape — no `"realm:binding"` string form. Wave-b deleted
`parse` and `Display` on both the core type and the wire projection so
the colon-joined form cannot travel across wire boundaries.
WireAuthBindingRef.title: WireAuthBindingRef

WireAuthProfile.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::AuthProfile`]. Sensitive credential
material is NOT wire-projected; callers that inspect profile metadata use
the server-side `auth.profile.get` RPC or the
`GET /auth/bindings/{binding_id}` REST path; both return typed redacted
shapes. `source_kind` is a
discriminator for the credential-source variant.
WireAuthProfile.title: WireAuthProfile

WireAuthProfilesList.description:
`GET /auth/profiles` success body — realm-scoped lists of backend,
auth, and binding profiles.
WireAuthProfilesList.title: WireAuthProfilesList

WireAuthStatusDetail.description:
`GET /auth/bindings/{binding_id}/status` success body. Richer than
[`WireAuthStatus`] — also carries `realm_id` / `binding_id` /
`auth_binding` / `has_refresh_token` so the caller can key by
binding directly.
WireAuthStatusDetail.title: WireAuthStatusDetail

provider.description:
Provider as a normalized string: `"openai"` / `"anthropic"` /
`"gemini"` / `"self_hosted"`. Wire types don't carry the strict
`Provider` enum so consumers aren't forced to update their
schema when we add new providers.

oneOf.description: Forward-compatibility for unknown block types.

Identifies a binding inside a realm on the wire. Shared by every
auth REST response that returns a `{realm_id, binding_id, auth_binding}`
trio. Built from a typed [`meerkat_core::AuthBindingRef`] so the three
fields always agree; the `realm_id`/`binding_id` strings carry the
slug form for wire consumers that key by string.

WireError.description:
Canonical wire error envelope.

Surfaces map this to their native format (RPC error, HTTP response, CLI exit).
WireError.title: WireError

WireLoginReady.description:
`POST /auth/login/complete` / ready leg of device-code success body.

The optional `state` field distinguishes the flat `POST
/auth/login/complete` response (no `state` set) from the device-code
ready leg (`state = "ready"`) which is part of the pending/slow_down/
access_denied/expired/ready tagged protocol.
WireLoginReady.title: WireLoginReady

WireModelProfile.description: Runtime profile for a model — capabilities and parameter schema.

beta_headers.description: Beta headers authorized by the model capability catalog.

inline_video.description: Whether the model accepts inline video content in user messages.

params_schema.description: JSON Schema describing accepted provider-specific parameters.

supports_reasoning.description: Whether the model supports explicit reasoning effort control.

supports_temperature.description: Whether the model accepts a `temperature` parameter.

supports_thinking.description: Whether the model supports extended thinking.

supports_web_search.description: Whether the model supports provider-native web search tools.

oneOf.description: Tested and recommended for production use.
Supported but not the primary recommendation.

WireProviderMeta.description: Provider continuity metadata for transcript blocks.

WireRealmConnectionSet.description:
Wire projection of [`meerkat_core::RealmConnectionSet`]. Returned
from the `realm/get` / `GET /realm/:id` endpoints.
WireRealmConnectionSet.title: WireRealmConnectionSet

WireRealtimeAttachmentStatus.description: Public live attachment status projection used by runtime and mob surfaces.

WireRunResult.description: Canonical run result for wire protocol.
WireRunResult.title: WireRunResult

WireRuntimeState.description: Public runtime state projection used by RPC surfaces.

WireSessionHistory.description:
Full session history in canonical wire format.

Not `PartialEq`: `messages` carries `WireSessionMessage` which
transitively holds opaque tool-call args; compare via serialization
round-trip rather than field equality.
WireSessionHistory.title: WireSessionHistory

WireSessionMessage.description:
Canonical transcript message for public wire surfaces.

Not `PartialEq`: the `BlockAssistant.blocks` variant carries
`WireAssistantBlock`s which hold opaque tool-call args as
`Box<RawValue>`. See `WireAssistantBlock` doc.

WireSessionSummary.description: Canonical session summary for wire protocol.
WireSessionSummary.title: WireSessionSummary

WireStopReason.description: Canonical stop reason for transcript messages.

WireToolCall.description:
Legacy assistant tool call payload.

Not `PartialEq`: `args` rides as `Box<RawValue>` for pass-through
fidelity with `meerkat_core::types::AssistantBlock::ToolUse.args` —
opaque from provider to dispatcher, never re-parsed on the wire.
Equivalence checks should round-trip through serialization.

WireToolResult.description: Tool result payload in a transcript.

WireToolResultContent.description: Wire-safe tool result content that handles both legacy string and array formats.

WireUsage.description: Canonical token usage for wire protocol.

post.summary: Finish OAuth login with an authorization code

get.summary: List realm auth profiles, backend profiles, and bindings

post.summary: Fork a helper member in a mob
Respawn a mob member with topology restore

get.description: Returns the current execution/status snapshot for the named mob member.
get.summary: Get a mob member execution status snapshot

post.summary: Spawn a helper member in a mob
Get product-layer realtime capabilities for a target
Get bootstrap metadata for opening a realtime channel
Get product-layer realtime channel status for a target
Create and run a new session
Admit a correlated terminal peer response through the typed runtime ingress

get.summary: Get a session's realtime attachment status
Get a session's current runtime state

post.summary: Append system context to a session
